intro: Erica stelleriana, also known as Steller's heather, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to the mountainous regions of the Pacific Northwest. Traditionally, Steller's heather has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Erica stelleriana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Steller's heather is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
